NKJV.1: A Song of Ascents. Unto You I lift up my eyes, O You who dwell in the heavens.
NKJV.2: Behold, as the eyes of servants look to the hand of their masters, As the eyes of a maid to the hand of her mistress, So our eyes look to the LORD our God, Until He has mercy on us.
NKJV.3: Have mercy on us, O LORD, have mercy on us! For we are exceedingly filled with contempt.
NKJV.4: Our soul is exceedingly filled With the scorn of those who are at ease, With the contempt of the proud.

KJV.1: A Song<S>7892</S> of degrees<S>4609</S>. Unto thee lift I up<S>5375</S> mine eyes<S>5869</S>, O thou that dwellest<S>3427</S> in the heavens<S>8064</S>.

ESV.1: To you I lift up my eyes, O you who are enthroned in the heavens!
ESV.2: Behold, as the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, as the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress, so our eyes look to the LORD our God, till he has mercy upon us.
ESV.3: Have mercy upon us, O LORD, have mercy upon us, for we have had more than enough of contempt.
ESV.4: Our soul has had more than enough of the scorn of those who are at ease, of the contempt of the proud.

NASB.1: A Song of Ascents. To You I lift up my eyes, O You who are enthroned in the heavens!
NASB.2: Behold, as the eyes of servants [look] to the hand of their master, As the eyes of a maid to the hand of her mistress, So our eyes [look] to the LORD our God, Until He is gracious to us.
NASB.3: Be gracious to us, O LORD, be gracious to us, For we are greatly filled with contempt.
NASB.4: Our soul is greatly filled With the scoffing of those who are at ease, [And] with the contempt of the proud.

NLT.1:
I lift my eyes to you,
O God, enthroned in heaven.
NLT.2:
We keep looking to the LORD our God for his mercy,
just as servants keep their eyes on their master,
as a slave girl watches her mistress for the slightest signal.
NLT.3:
Have mercy on us, LORD, have mercy,
for we have had our fill of contempt.
NLT.4:
We have had more than our fill of the scoffing of the proud
and the contempt of the arrogant.

WEB.1: A Song of Ascents. To you I do lift up my eyes, you who sit in the heavens.
WEB.2: Behold, as the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, as the eyes of a maid to the hand of her mistress; so our eyes look to Yahweh, our God, until he has mercy on us.
WEB.3: Have mercy on us, Yahweh, have mercy on us, for we have endured much contempt.
WEB.4: Our soul is exceedingly filled with the scoffing of those who are at ease, with the contempt of the proud.

CSB17.1:
A song of ascents. [1] I lift my eyes to you,
the one enthroned in heaven. ⓗ
CSB17.2:
Like a servant’s eyes on his master’s hand,
like a servant girl’s eyes on her mistress’s hand,
so our eyes are on the LORD our God
until he shows us favor. ⓘ
CSB17.3:
Show us favor, LORD, show us favor,
for we’ve had more than enough contempt. ⓙ
CSB17.4:
We’ve had more than enough
scorn from the arrogant
and contempt from the proud. ⓚ

AMP.1:
A Song of Ascents. Unto you I lift up my eyes,
O You who are enthroned in the heavens!
AMP.2:
Behold, as the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master,
And as the eyes of a maid to the hand of her mistress,
So our eyes look to the LORD our God,
Until He is gracious and favorable toward us.
AMP.3:
Be gracious to us, O LORD, be gracious and favorable toward us,
For we are greatly filled with contempt.
AMP.4:
Our soul is greatly filled
With the scoffing of those who are at ease,
And with the contempt of the proud [who disregard God's law].

MSG.1:
A pilgrim song
I look to you, heaven-dwelling God,
look up to you for help.
MSG.2:
Like servants, alert to their master's commands,
like a maiden attending her lady,
We're watching and waiting, holding our breath,
awaiting your word of mercy.
MSG.3:
Mercy, God, mercy!
We've been kicked around long enough,
MSG.4:
Kicked in the teeth by complacent rich men,
kicked when we're down by arrogant brutes.

NIV.1:
Psalm 123
A song of ascents.
I lift up my eyes to you,
to you whose throne is in heaven.
NIV.2:
As the eyes of slaves look to the hand of their master,
as the eyes of a maid look to the hand of her mistress,
so our eyes look to the Lord our God,
till he shows us his mercy.
NIV.3:
Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us,
for we have endured much contempt.
NIV.4:
We have endured much ridicule from the proud,
much contempt from the arrogant.

NRSVCE.1:
To you I lift up my eyes,
O you who are enthroned in the heavens!
NRSVCE.2:
As the eyes of servants
look to the hand of their master,
as the eyes of a maid
to the hand of her mistress,
so our eyes look to the LORD our God,
until he has mercy upon us.
NRSVCE.3:
Have mercy upon us, O LORD, have mercy upon us,
for we have had more than enough of contempt.
NRSVCE.4:
Our soul has had more than its fill
of the scorn of those who are at ease,
of the contempt of the proud.

NET.1: I look up toward you, the one enthroned in heaven.
NET.2: Look, as the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, as the eyes of a female servant look to the hand of her mistress, so my eyes will look to the LORD, our God, until he shows us favor.
NET.3: Show us favor, O LORD, show us favor! For we have had our fill of humiliation, and then some.
NET.4: We have had our fill of the taunts of the self-assured, of the contempt of the proud.
